meaning: n.刺激,兴奋,激动,激励,鼓舞,[物]激发,励磁,[植]激感(现象)
examples: The effects of coherence on excitation transfer are also discussed.
The above-mentioned act is owed to ghastfulness, consternation, excitation or excessively panic-stricken.
The excitation energy changes the way each molecule absorbs and emits light.
Or is it the excitation of the senses that forms the preamble for the box spring bustle?
Many of them are breathtakingly direct, but what the bluntness supports, often, is irony rather than excitation.
